summary: This page presents Fig. 1.3 with several signals used in Exercise E1.1 and concludes calculations for signal power and rms value, including a complex exponential signal whose power is |D|^2. It also comments on when powers of summed sinusoids can be added and introduces exercises on energy, power, rms value, and the special case of equal-frequency sinusoids.

figures.caption: Five plots of different signals labeled (a) through (e). (a) f1(t) is a rectangular pulse. (b) f2(t) is a rectangular pulse. (c) f3(t) is a ramp signal. (d) f4(t) is a ramp signal. (e) f5(t) is a periodic signal composed of decaying exponentials.
